category_sources: Official Invisible Island luit page describes luit as a filter between an arbitrary application and a UTF-8 terminal emulator.
source_facts.description: Filter run between arbitrary application and UTF-8 terminal emulator.
display_name_sources: Official Invisible Island page heading uses luit.
docs_sources: Official Invisible Island luit page has a Documentation section linking to the luit program manual.
repo_sources: Official page notes Xorg repositories as older or inactive related sources, not the current official source-control repository.
source_facts.repo is empty; official Invisible Island page provides source archives but no current official source-control repository for the maintained project.
tags_sources: Official Invisible Island page says luit converts application output from locale encoding to UTF-8 and terminal input back to locale encoding, mainly for xterm.
